rules.text: Check BMP at 1 week post-initiation of ARNI or MRA in HFrEF patients.

attributes.Rationale: High risk of hyperkalemia and AKI in patients with baseline CKD [WikiID: 2f6961].

content:
- Check BMP at 1 week post-initiation of ARNI or MRA in HFrEF patients.
  - Rationale: High risk of hyperkalemia and AKI in patients with baseline CKD [WikiID: 2f6961].
  - Evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline [WikiID: 2f6961]

rules.text: Limit initial fluid bolus to 20mL/kg in patients with concomitant HF or ESRD.

attributes.Rationale: Standard 30mL/kg bolus carries a high risk of volume overload in susceptible populations [WikiID: d41bc0].

content:
- Limit initial fluid bolus to 20mL/kg in patients with concomitant HF or ESRD.
  - Rationale: Standard 30mL/kg bolus carries a high risk of volume overload in susceptible populations [WikiID: d41bc0].
  - Evidence: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 [WikiID: d41bc0]

rules.text: For symptomatic AFib in patients >80 years old, defer rhythm control preference to cardiology or accept rate control as an alternative.

attributes.Rationale: Early rhythm control is preferred generally, but clinical nuance is required for the elderly to balance symptom control vs. procedure risk [WikiID: c94385].

content:
- For symptomatic AFib in patients >80 years old, defer rhythm control preference to cardiology or accept rate control as an alternative.
  - Rationale: Early rhythm control is preferred generally, but clinical nuance is required for the elderly to balance symptom control vs. procedure risk [WikiID: c94385].
  - Evidence: 2023 ACC/AHA AFib Guideline [WikiID: c94385]

rules.text: In patients with HFrEF and anemia (Hgb < 12 g/dL), routinely order iron studies (ferritin, TSAT) and TSH as part of the precipitant/comorbidity workup.

attributes.Rationale: Anemia and thyroid dysfunction may contribute to symptoms or exacerbate HFrEF; evaluating these factors aids in comprehensive management and identifying potential triggers for recurrent admissions.
attributes.Evidence: General clinical practice for recurrent HF decompensation [WikiID: b9f1b7, WikiID: 6bddc0].

content:
- In patients with HFrEF and anemia (Hgb < 12 g/dL), routinely order iron studies (ferritin, TSAT) and TSH as part of the precipitant/comorbidity workup.
  - Rationale: Anemia and thyroid dysfunction may contribute to symptoms or exacerbate HFrEF; evaluating these factors aids in comprehensive management and identifying potential triggers for recurrent admissions.
  - Evidence: General clinical practice for recurrent HF decompensation [WikiID: b9f1b7, WikiID: 6bddc0].

rules.text: For patients with a documented severe sulfa allergy (hives/anaphylaxis), perform a documented safety check for potential cross-reactivity with sulfonamide-derivative diuretics (e.g., Furosemide) before administration.

attributes.Rationale: Standardization of safety screening to prevent potential allergic reactions.
attributes.Interpretation: If cross-reactivity is suspected, evaluate for alternative non-sulfonamide loop diuretics like ethacrynic acid [WikiID: 410372].

content:
- For patients with a documented severe sulfa allergy (hives/anaphylaxis), perform a documented safety check for potential cross-reactivity with sulfonamide-derivative diuretics (e.g., Furosemide) before administration.
  - Rationale: Standardization of safety screening to prevent potential allergic reactions.
  - Interpretation: If cross-reactivity is suspected, evaluate for alternative non-sulfonamide loop diuretics like ethacrynic acid [WikiID: 410372].

rules.text: In the Assessment & Plan, specify explicit thresholds for downstream actions (e.g., 'start spironolactone when Cr stable and K <5.0') rather than using vague, deferred language.

attributes.Rationale: Reduces clinical ambiguity and ensures clear, actionable plan-of-care for the multidisciplinary team [WikiID: cfacb2].

content:
- In the Assessment & Plan, specify explicit thresholds for downstream actions (e.g., 'start spironolactone when Cr stable and K <5.0') rather than using vague, deferred language.
  - Rationale: Reduces clinical ambiguity and ensures clear, actionable plan-of-care for the multidisciplinary team [WikiID: cfacb2].

header: Heart failure exacerbation - target dry weight setting

rules.text: When dry-weight creep is observed across admissions, set the new target dry weight below the most recent discharge weight (e.g., 3-4 kg below) rather than anchoring to the last documented dry weight.

attributes.Rationale: Anchoring to a creeping dry weight perpetuates inadequate decongestion; using a lower target accounts for likely under-diuresis on prior admissions.

content:
- When dry-weight creep is observed across admissions, set the new target dry weight below the most recent discharge weight (e.g., 3-4 kg below) rather than anchoring to the last documented dry weight.
  - Rationale: Anchoring to a creeping dry weight perpetuates inadequate decongestion; using a lower target accounts for likely under-diuresis on prior admissions.

rules.text: For recurrent admissions where patient reports strict adherence, verify true adherence with pill counts and pharmacy refill history rather than relying on self-report alone.

attributes.Rationale: Self-reported adherence is unreliable in recurrent decompensations; objective verification is needed before attributing precipitant to non-adherence vs. other causes.

content:
- For recurrent admissions where patient reports strict adherence, verify true adherence with pill counts and pharmacy refill history rather than relying on self-report alone.
  - Rationale: Self-reported adherence is unreliable in recurrent decompensations; objective verification is needed before attributing precipitant to non-adherence vs. other causes.

rules.text: When carvedilol or other beta-blockers are continued in T2DM patients on sliding-scale insulin, instruct nursing to monitor glucose objectively (fingersticks QID) rather than relying on adrenergic hypoglycemia symptoms.

attributes.Rationale: Beta-blockers mask adrenergic symptoms of hypoglycemia, increasing risk of unrecognized hypoglycemic events.

content:
- When carvedilol or other beta-blockers are continued in T2DM patients on sliding-scale insulin, instruct nursing to monitor glucose objectively (fingersticks QID) rather than relying on adrenergic hypoglycemia symptoms.
  - Rationale: Beta-blockers mask adrenergic symptoms of hypoglycemia, increasing risk of unrecognized hypoglycemic events.

rules.text: If switching from ACEi to ARNI per cardiology recommendation, require a 36-hour washout period and initiate Part D prior authorization workflow at least 48h before anticipated discharge.

attributes.Rationale: Concomitant ACEi/ARNI use causes angioedema risk; ARNI also has high PA likelihood under Medicare Part D and is a common cause of discharge delay.

content:
- If switching from ACEi to ARNI per cardiology recommendation, require a 36-hour washout period and initiate Part D prior authorization workflow at least 48h before anticipated discharge.
  - Rationale: Concomitant ACEi/ARNI use causes angioedema risk; ARNI also has high PA likelihood under Medicare Part D and is a common cause of discharge delay.

header: Loop diuretic safety in sulfa allergy

rules.text: When a patient with documented sulfa allergy has already received furosemide in the ED without a cross-reactivity check, do not defer the safety check — verify with pharmacy/attending BEFORE the next scheduled dose, and switch to ethacrynic acid if true reaction history.

attributes.Rationale: Continuing furosemide pending later review exposes the patient to repeated allergen doses; cross-reactivity must be resolved pre-dose, not flagged for later.

content:
- When a patient with documented sulfa allergy has already received furosemide in the ED without a cross-reactivity check, do not defer the safety check — verify with pharmacy/attending BEFORE the next scheduled dose, and switch to ethacrynic acid if true reaction history.
  - Rationale: Continuing furosemide pending later review exposes the patient to repeated allergen doses; cross-reactivity must be resolved pre-dose, not flagged for later.

rules.text: Before sign-off, check that note drafts are not truncated mid-sentence or contain unclosed brackets/sections; flag incomplete drafts explicitly in safety review.

attributes.Rationale: Truncated notes may omit safety-critical content (e.g., allergy lists, plan items) and pose medico-legal and clinical risk.

content:
- Before sign-off, check that note drafts are not truncated mid-sentence or contain unclosed brackets/sections; flag incomplete drafts explicitly in safety review.
  - Rationale: Truncated notes may omit safety-critical content (e.g., allergy lists, plan items) and pose medico-legal and clinical risk.

rules.text: Each home medication in the admission note should be annotated inline with explicit continue/hold/dose-adjust decision AND the specific numerical hold threshold parameters (e.g., 'Hold if SBP <100, Cr >2.0, or K+ >5.0').

attributes.Rationale: Explicit numerical thresholds prevent ambiguity for covering clinicians and nursing staff regarding when to hold the next dose.

content:
- Each home medication in the admission note should be annotated inline with explicit continue/hold/dose-adjust decision AND the specific numerical hold threshold parameters (e.g., 'Hold if SBP <100, Cr >2.0, or K+ >5.0').
  - Rationale: Explicit numerical thresholds prevent ambiguity for covering clinicians and nursing staff regarding when to hold the next dose.

rules.text: Prescription agent_notes should include: dose rationale (e.g., 2x home PO for IV loop), explicit hold/escalation triggers with numerical thresholds, discharge transition plan (when to resume home regimen), and PA workflow timing for high-risk agents.

attributes.Rationale: Consolidating dose logic, safety thresholds, and discharge planning per-medication reduces handoff errors and discharge delays.

content:
- Prescription agent_notes should include: dose rationale (e.g., 2x home PO for IV loop), explicit hold/escalation triggers with numerical thresholds, discharge transition plan (when to resume home regimen), and PA workflow timing for high-risk agents.
  - Rationale: Consolidating dose logic, safety thresholds, and discharge planning per-medication reduces handoff errors and discharge delays.

rules.text: When wiki drift audits encounter conflicting signals (e.g., 'no wiki loaded' but wiki content visible in context), explicitly flag the discrepancy and request physician confirmation rather than proceeding with a PASS verdict.

attributes.Rationale: Defaulting to PASS on ambiguous source state risks missing actual drift; physician must confirm authoritative wiki version.

content:
- When wiki drift audits encounter conflicting signals (e.g., 'no wiki loaded' but wiki content visible in context), explicitly flag the discrepancy and request physician confirmation rather than proceeding with a PASS verdict.
  - Rationale: Defaulting to PASS on ambiguous source state risks missing actual drift; physician must confirm authoritative wiki version.

- Formulary PA flags on standard inpatient agents (e.g., heparin SQ, insulin lispro) are typically Part D outpatient lookup artifacts — these agents are covered under inpatient Part A DRG bundling and do not require PA inpatient.
  - Rationale: Avoid unnecessary PA workflows for bundled inpatient medications; reserve PA effort for true outpatient-transition agents (ARNI, SGLT2i).
  - Interpretation: Annotate pa_notes to clarify Part A vs Part D distinction rather than triggering PA workflow.

rules.text: Formulary PA flags on standard inpatient agents (e.g., heparin SQ, insulin lispro) are typically Part D outpatient lookup artifacts — these agents are covered under inpatient Part A DRG bundling and do not require PA inpatient.

attributes.Rationale: Avoid unnecessary PA workflows for bundled inpatient medications; reserve PA effort for true outpatient-transition agents (ARNI, SGLT2i).
attributes.Interpretation: Annotate pa_notes to clarify Part A vs Part D distinction rather than triggering PA workflow.

header: Sulfa allergy and home loop diuretic tolerance
content:
- When a patient with documented sulfa allergy has tolerated home furosemide chronically, this supports clinical cross-reactivity tolerance — document this reasoning when proceeding with inpatient furosemide.
  - Rationale: Prior tolerance of home dose is meaningful clinical evidence against clinically significant cross-reactivity, even when allergy is documented.
  - Interpretation: Still requires pharmacy/attending verification before escalated IV dosing, but home tolerance reduces likelihood of reaction.

rules.text: When a patient with documented sulfa allergy has tolerated home furosemide chronically, this supports clinical cross-reactivity tolerance — document this reasoning when proceeding with inpatient furosemide.

attributes.Rationale: Prior tolerance of home dose is meaningful clinical evidence against clinically significant cross-reactivity, even when allergy is documented.
attributes.Interpretation: Still requires pharmacy/attending verification before escalated IV dosing, but home tolerance reduces likelihood of reaction.

content:
- When insulin sliding scale is used in patients on concurrent loop diuretic, monitor potassium daily — insulin shifts K+ intracellularly, additive with diuretic-induced K+ losses.
  - Rationale: Compounded hypokalemia risk increases arrhythmia potential, especially in HFrEF.
  - Diagnostic Questions: Is the patient on a beta-blocker that may mask hypoglycemia symptoms?

rules.text: When insulin sliding scale is used in patients on concurrent loop diuretic, monitor potassium daily — insulin shifts K+ intracellularly, additive with diuretic-induced K+ losses.

attributes.Rationale: Compounded hypokalemia risk increases arrhythmia potential, especially in HFrEF.
attributes.Diagnostic Questions: Is the patient on a beta-blocker that may mask hypoglycemia symptoms?

content:
- When formulary lookup flags PA on a standard inpatient agent, explicitly explain in pa_notes that the flag reflects outpatient Part D lookup and the agent is covered under inpatient Part A DRG bundling.
  - Rationale: Prevents false-positive PA workflows on bundled inpatient meds and clarifies the artifact for downstream reviewers.

rules.text: When formulary lookup flags PA on a standard inpatient agent, explicitly explain in pa_notes that the flag reflects outpatient Part D lookup and the agent is covered under inpatient Part A DRG bundling.

attributes.Rationale: Prevents false-positive PA workflows on bundled inpatient meds and clarifies the artifact for downstream reviewers.

content:
- Wiki drift checks should also surface internal Wiki consistency tensions (e.g., a deferral rule conflicting with an adopted guideline), not only physician-vs-guideline drift.
  - Rationale: Internal contradictions create plan ambiguity at the bedside; surfacing them prompts explicit threshold-based deferrals per existing documentation standards.

rules.text: Wiki drift checks should also surface internal Wiki consistency tensions (e.g., a deferral rule conflicting with an adopted guideline), not only physician-vs-guideline drift.

attributes.Rationale: Internal contradictions create plan ambiguity at the bedside; surfacing them prompts explicit threshold-based deferrals per existing documentation standards.

content:
- When flagging guideline drift, cite the specific landmark trial(s) that update the evidence base (e.g., EAST-AFNET 4 for early rhythm control, CLOVERS/CLASSIC for restrictive sepsis fluids).
  - Rationale: Trial-level citations give the physician actionable grounds to revise stored reasoning, not just guideline reference.

rules.text: When flagging guideline drift, cite the specific landmark trial(s) that update the evidence base (e.g., EAST-AFNET 4 for early rhythm control, CLOVERS/CLASSIC for restrictive sepsis fluids).

attributes.Rationale: Trial-level citations give the physician actionable grounds to revise stored reasoning, not just guideline reference.

content:
- If a document under review is truncated mid-section, explicitly flag the truncation and list which mandatory sections (A&P, problem list, code status, VTE ppx, orders) are not visible — do not infer their presence or absence.
  - Rationale: Prevents false-pass safety clearance and forces the physician to supply the complete document before sign-off.

rules.text: If a document under review is truncated mid-section, explicitly flag the truncation and list which mandatory sections (A&P, problem list, code status, VTE ppx, orders) are not visible — do not infer their presence or absence.
